title: Validate an Employee ID for Payroll System

content.problemStatement:
In this task, you are required to clean and validate an employee ID by removing unwanted spaces, converting it to uppercase, and checking if it contains only numeric characters.

**Data Values**


`employee_id = "  e123"`
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T (1 ≤ T ≤ 10), the number of test cases. Each of the next T lines contains a string representing an employee ID. The string may contain leading, trailing, or internal spaces.
content.outputFormat:
For each test case output two lines:
1. The cleaned employee ID after removing all spaces and converting all letters to uppercase.
2. "True" if the cleaned ID consists only of numeric characters, otherwise "False".

sampleTestCases.explanation: Spaces are removed, the remaining string "e123" is converted to uppercase "E123". It contains a non‑digit character, so the validation result is False.
First ID becomes "00123" (all digits) → True. Second ID stays "ABC456" (letters present) → False.
1) "987" → digits only. 2) "a b c" → "abc" → "ABC" → not all digits. 3) "12 34" → "1234" → all digits.

hidden.label: general case 1 - mixed letters and digits

solution.approach: For each test case read the whole line (including spaces). Iterate over the characters, skip spaces, convert every other character to uppercase and append to a new string. After the cleaning step, check whether the resulting string is non‑empty and every character is a decimal digit. Print the cleaned string and the boolean result. The algorithm is linear in the length of the input string.
solution.timeComplexity: O(L) per test case, where L is the length of the raw input line (≤ 100). Overall O(T·L).
solution.spaceComplexity: O(L) for the cleaned string per test case.
